page.title: Spanish Constitution Day Yacht Charter in Marbella: A Long Weekend at Sea

data.meta_description: Celebrate Spanish Constitution Day with a yacht charter in Marbella. Enjoy a long weekend at sea with our fleet from Puerto Banús. Book now.
data.summary: Plan your long weekend around Spanish Constitution Day (6 December) with a yacht charter from Marbella. Our fleet departs Puerto Banús with skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks included.
key_facts: Spanish Constitution Day is 6 December; a public holiday in Spain.
Long weekend usually runs Saturday–Monday or Friday–Monday.
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella.
Skipper, fuel, drinks (beer, wine, cava, soft drinks), snacks, insurance, and VAT included.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749 for 2 hours to €2,299 for 8 hours.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) from €4,719 for 4 hours, includes a jet ski.
Other boats (e.g., Ferretti 94, Canados 86) – pricing on request via WhatsApp.
December sea temperatures ~16°C; sunny days common but pack a jacket.

faq.a: Spanish Constitution Day (Día de la Constitución) is a national public holiday on 6 December, commemorating the 1978 referendum that approved Spain's current constitution. It often creates a long weekend, especially when it falls near a weekend or the Immaculate Conception holiday on 8 December.

faq.q: Can I charter a yacht during the Constitution Day long weekend?
faq.a: Yes, our boats operate year-round from Puerto Banús. Availability is good in early December, but we recommend booking at least two weeks ahead. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance.

faq.q: Which boats are available for a Constitution Day charter?
faq.a: Our fleet includes 17 boats from 6.4m to 29m. For a small group, the Mariah SX21 (5 pax) or Dubhe (5 pax) work well. For up to 12 guests, choose the Mangusta 80, Azimut 58 Flybridge, or Ferretti 94. Pricing is tiered – see our fleet page for details.

faq.q: What is the weather like in Marbella in early December?
faq.a: Marbella enjoys mild winters. Average highs are 17–19°C, lows around 10°C. Sea temperature is about 16°C – not swimming weather for most, but perfect for cruising along the coast with a jacket. Sunny days are common.

faq.q: How long is a typical charter during the long weekend?
faq.a: Most guests book half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours) charters. A 4-hour trip can reach Cabopino or Estepona. An 8-hour charter allows time for lunch at a beach club, a stop at Cala del Faro, or a run to Sotogrande.

faq.q: Is food provided on the yacht?
faq.a: Light snacks (chips, olives, nuts) are included. You are welcome to bring your own food or order a catering platter in advance. Many guests buy fresh seafood from the Puerto Banús market or order from a local deli.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for Constitution Day?
faq.a: You can book directly on our website or contact us via WhatsApp. We’ll confirm availability, recommend a boat for your group size, and send a payment link. A 50% deposit secures the date.

faq.q: What if the weather is bad on the day?
faq.a: We monitor forecasts closely. If conditions are unsafe, we offer a full refund or reschedule at no extra cost. December can bring occasional rain or wind, but Marbella's sheltered coast usually allows cruising.

data.body_html:
Spanish Constitution Day (6 December) marks the 1978 referendum that approved Spain's current democratic constitution. For many, it creates a long weekend — especially when it falls near the Immaculate Conception holiday on 8 December. It's the perfect excuse to escape the winter chill and spend a day on the Mediterranean aboard a Boat Rental Marbella yacht. Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús, and every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT. Whether you want a quiet cruise or a party on the water, we have a boat for you.

Why Charter a Yacht for Constitution Day?

December in Marbella is mild — average highs of 18°C, sea temperatures around 16°C, and plenty of sunshine. A yacht charter lets you see the coast from a new perspective without the summer crowds. The long weekend means you can take a half-day trip to Cabopino or Estepona, or make a full day of it with lunch at a beach club. It's also a great way to celebrate with family or colleagues before the Christmas rush.

Our Fleet for the Long Weekend

We operate 17 boats from Puerto Banús, all maintained to high standards. Here are the most popular choices for a Constitution Day charter:

  • Tier A (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39): €749 for 2 hours, €2,299 for 8 hours. Ideal for groups of 9–11. Both are 12.5m motor yachts with comfortable salons and sun decks.
  • Tier B (Mangusta 80, including white and grey variants): From €4,719 for 4 hours, includes a jet ski. 24m open yacht, 12 pax. Perfect for a stylish day out.
  • Other boats: For larger groups, consider the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) or Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax). Pricing is on request via WhatsApp.
  • Smaller boats: The Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax) or Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) are budget-friendly options for couples or small families.

All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, insurance, and 21% Spanish VAT.

Recommended Itineraries for a December Charter

With shorter daylight hours (sunset around 18:00), we suggest these routes:

  • 4-hour trip: Head west to Estepona (8 NM) for views of the Sierra Bermeja mountains, or east to Cabopino (6 NM) for a quiet anchorage near the dunes.
  • 8-hour trip: Go further west to Sotogrande (20 NM) for a lunch stop at a marina restaurant, or east to Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés for a swim (if you're brave — water is 16°C).
  • Sunset cruise: Stay close to Marbella Marina and the Golden Mile, watching the lights come on along the promenade.

All routes include the option to anchor for a drink and snacks. Your skipper will tailor the day to your preferences.

What’s Included in Your Charter

Every booking includes:

  • Licensed, English-speaking skipper
  • Fuel for the full itinerary
  • Unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (chips, olives, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • 21% Spanish IVA

You are welcome to bring your own food or arrange catering. Many guests buy fresh seafood from the market at Puerto Banús. We can also recommend local caterers for a bespoke menu.

Booking Tips for the Long Weekend

December is a quiet season for yacht charters, so you'll have more choice. However, we recommend booking at least two weeks in advance to secure your preferred boat. A 50% deposit is required to confirm. If the weather turns bad, we offer a full refund or reschedule. Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing on larger yachts.

For more inspiration, read our guide to Spanish Constitution Day yacht charter Marbella and explore other long-weekend ideas on our blog.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is Spanish Constitution Day?

Spanish Constitution Day (Día de la Constitución) is a national public holiday on 6 December, commemorating the 1978 referendum that approved Spain's current constitution. It often creates a long weekend, especially when it falls near a weekend or the Immaculate Conception holiday on 8 December.

Can I charter a yacht during the Constitution Day long weekend?

Yes, our boats operate year-round from Puerto Banús. Availability is good in early December, but we recommend booking at least two weeks ahead. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance.

Which boats are available for a Constitution Day charter?

Our fleet includes 17 boats from 6.4m to 29m. For a small group, the Mariah SX21 (5 pax) or Dubhe (5 pax) work well. For up to 12 guests, choose the Mangusta 80, Azimut 58 Flybridge, or Ferretti 94. Pricing is tiered – see our fleet page for details.

What is the weather like in Marbella in early December?

Marbella enjoys mild winters. Average highs are 17–19°C, lows around 10°C. Sea temperature is about 16°C – not swimming weather for most, but perfect for cruising along the coast with a jacket. Sunny days are common.

How long is a typical charter during the long weekend?

Most guests book half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours) charters. A 4-hour trip can reach Cabopino or Estepona. An 8-hour charter allows time for lunch at a beach club, a stop at Cala del Faro, or a run to Sotogrande.

Is food provided on the yacht?

Light snacks (chips, olives, nuts) are included. You are welcome to bring your own food or order a catering platter in advance. Many guests buy fresh seafood from the Puerto Banús market or order from a local deli.

How do I book a yacht for Constitution Day?

You can book directly on our website or contact us via WhatsApp. We’ll confirm availability, recommend a boat for your group size, and send a payment link. A 50% deposit secures the date.

What if the weather is bad on the day?

We monitor forecasts closely. If conditions are unsafe, we offer a full refund or reschedule at no extra cost. December can bring occasional rain or wind, but Marbella's sheltered coast usually allows cruising.
